Before V2.84, a real worker timeout created a same-scope debug task, and a debug timeout reset the parent task for replay. Billing Core run_attempt_019 reproduced this as T002 timeout, T002-DEBUG-1 timeout, then T002 replay; V2.84 records a non-partial technical blocker instead.

Before V2.85, full-roadmap resume selection treated active-task attempts with terminal timeout lifecycle evidence as resumable if no worker PID was alive. V2.85 skips those stale terminal-active attempts and starts a fresh attempt.

Before V2.86, manifest-scoped frontend tasks could fail Alchemy boundary audit when dependency commands touched same-directory lockfiles such as frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml. V2.86 expands allowed_files from package.json to common lockfile companions.

Before V2.87, a manually stopped debug worker could leave active_tasks pointing at T002-DEBUG-1 with a dead running lifecycle record, causing the next Billing Core resume to replay stale debug work. V2.87 skips dead active debug attempts while preserving ordinary interrupted task resume.

V2.122 also protects later split verification resumes: if a split task such as T023 `Verify frontend tests` times out, the next graph starts from that split title instead of replaying backend tests.

V2.132 adds only one bounded progress-grace window for active verification/build child processes. If a task still exceeds the original timeout plus grace, Alchemy should split the task further rather than globally increasing worker budgets.
